metadata.description: Run any Vision Language Model with fal. Analyze and understand images using Claude (Anthropic), GPT-5 / GPT-4o (OpenAI), Gemini (Google), Grok (xAI), Llama (Meta), Qwen, Pixtral (Mistral), and more. Send one or multiple images for captioning, analysis, OCR, or visual Q&A. Powered by OpenRouter.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for openrouter/router/vision
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the openrouter/router/vision queue.

x-fal-metadata.about: Run any VLM (Vision Language Model) with fal, powered by OpenRouter.

reasoning.title: Reasoning
reasoning.description: Should reasoning be the part of the final answer.

model.title: Model
model.description: Name of the model to use. Charged based on actual token usage.

system_prompt.description: System prompt to provide context or instructions to the model
system_prompt.title: System Prompt
examples: Only answer the question, do not provide any additional information or add any prefix/suffix other than the answer of the original question. Don't use markdown.

max_tokens.description: This sets the upper limit for the number of tokens the model can generate in response. It won't produce more than this limit. The maximum value is the context length minus the prompt length.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

prompt.title: Prompt
examples: Caption this image for a text-to-image model with as much detail as possible.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: This setting influences the variety in the model's responses. Lower values lead to more predictable and typical responses, while higher values encourage more diverse and less common responses. At 0, the model always gives the same response for a given input.

image_urls.description: List of image URLs to be processed

examples: A close-up of a tiger's face focusing on its bright orange iris and the area around its eye, with white fur eyebrows and a contrasting black and rich orange striped fur pattern. The word "FLUX" is overlaid in bold, white, brush-stroke styled text across the tiger's face.

metadata.description: Predict whether an image is NSFW or SFW.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/x-ailab/nsfw

x-fal-metadata.about:
This model performs binary classification to determine whether an input image is Safe For Work (SFW) or Not Safe For Work (NSFW).

It directly assigns each image to one of two categories:
- SFW: The image does not contain explicit, adult, or inappropriate content.
- NSFW: The image is likely to contain nudity, sexual content, or other forms of explicit material.

The model is optimized for high-confidence decisions, making it well-suited for use cases that require hard filtering or real-time moderation.

image_urls.description: List of image URLs to check. If more than 10 images are provided, only the first 10 will be checked.
image_urls.title: Image Urls

has_nsfw_concepts.description: List of booleans indicating if the image has an NSFW concept
has_nsfw_concepts.title: Has Nsfw Concepts

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for perceptron/isaac-01
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the perceptron/isaac-01 queue.

examples: Which car is trying to park into garage?
prompt.description: Prompt to be used for the image
prompt.title: Prompt

response_style.description:
Response style to be used for the image.

- text: Model will output text. Good for descriptions and captioning.
- box: Model will output a combination of text and bounding boxes. Good for
localization.
- point: Model will output a combination of text and points. Good for counting many
objects.
- polygon: Model will output a combination of text and polygons. Good for granular
segmentation.
response_style.title: Response Style

image_url.description: Image URL to be processed
image_url.title: Image Url

error.description: Error message if an error occurred
error.title: Error

examples:
To determine which car is trying to park into the garage, we need to carefully observe the positions and movements of the vehicles in the image.

1. **Identify the Vehicles**: 
   - There are three vehicles visible: a green truck, an orange car, and a brown car.
   - The green truck is positioned in front of the garage entrance.
   - The orange car is parked to the left of the green truck.
   - The brown car is parked to the right of the green truck.

2. **Analyze the Positions**:
   - The green truck is directly in front of the garage entrance, suggesting it is in the process of moving towards the entrance.
   - The orange car is parked parallel to the garage but is not in the path of the green truck.
   - The brown car is also parked parallel to the garage but is not in the path of the green truck.

3. **Determine the Action**:
   - Given the position of the green truck directly in front of the garage entrance, it is most likely that the green truck is trying to park into the garage.
   - The orange and brown cars are already parked and do not appear to be in motion or attempting to park.

Therefore, the car that is trying to park into the garage is the green truck.
output.title: Output

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the completion
completion_tokens.title: Completion Tokens

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt
prompt_tokens.title: Prompt Tokens

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

events.description: Time-ranged events parsed from the caption.
events.title: Events

text.description: Full post-thinking caption text (Scene + Events) as returned by the model.

text.description: Short description of the event (caption mode only).
text.title: Text

info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the perceptron/isaac-01/openai/v1/chat/completions queue.

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Answer questions from the images.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/moondream/batched
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/moondream/batched queue.

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of new tokens to generate
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

inputs.description: List of input prompts and image URLs
inputs.title: Input prompt & image pairs

model_id.description: Model ID to use for inference
model_id.title: Model ID

timings.description: Timings for different parts of the process
timings.title: Timings

image_url.description: URL of the image to be processed
image_url.title: Image URL

examples: Do you know who drew this painting?
prompt.description: Prompt to be used for the image
prompt.title: Prompt

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: MoonDreamNext Batch is a multimodal vision-language model for batch captioning.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/moondream-next/batch
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/moondream-next/batch queue.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Single prompt to apply to all images

images_data_url.title: Image URLs
images_data_url.description: List of image URLs to be processed (maximum 32 images)

captions_file.description: URL to the generated captions JSON file containing filename-caption pairs.

file_size.title: File Size
file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: The mime type of the file.

file_name.title: File Name
file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Sa2VA is an MLLM capable of question answering, visual prompt understanding, and dense object segmentation at both image and video levels

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/sa2va/4b/video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/sa2va/4b/video queue.

num_frames_to_sample.description: Number of frames to sample from the video. Defaults to 32 if not provided. Minimum 5, maximum 100.
num_frames_to_sample.title: Num Frames To Sample

prompt.description: Prompt to be used for the chat completion
examples: Could you please give me a brief description of the video? Please respond with interleaved segmentation masks for the corresponding parts of the answer.
prompt.title: Prompt

video_url.description: The URL of the input video.
video_url.title: Video Url

examples:
Two children

[SEG] are jumping on

a bed

[SEG] .<|im_end|>
output.title: Output

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
content_type.title: Content Type

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
file_size.title: File Size

url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.
url.title: Url

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.
file_name.title: File Name

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

examples: Could you please give me a brief description of the video? Please respond with interleaved segmentation masks for the corresponding parts of the answer.

metadata.description: Marlin is a 2B video VLM tuned for the two questions developers actually want to ask of their videos: what is happening, and when?

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/marlin/find
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/marlin/find queue.

video_url.description: URL of the video to search through.
video_url.title: Video Url

temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Only used when do_sample is true.
temperature.title: Temperature

do_sample.description: If true, sample with temperature/top_p; if false, use greedy decoding.
do_sample.title: Do Sample

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Natural-language description of the event or scene to find in the video.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling threshold. Only used when do_sample is true.
top_p.title: Top P

format_ok.description: True iff the output matched the trained "From X to Y." format.
format_ok.title: Format Ok

span.description: Detected (start, end) span in seconds, or null if the model could not locate the event.

text.description: Short description of the event (caption mode only).
text.title: Text

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Moondream2 is a highly efficient open-source vision language model that combines powerful image understanding capabilities with a remarkably small footprint.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/moondream2/point-object-detection
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/moondream2/point-object-detection queue.

object.title: Object
object.description: Object to be detected in the image

image_url.description: URL of the image to be processed
image_url.title: Image URL

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
content_type.title: Content Type

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
file_size.title: File Size

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.
file_name.title: File Name

width.description: The width of the image in pixels.
width.title: Width

height.description: The height of the image in pixels.
height.title: Height

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.
